question: My crewmate is showing signs of a serious allergic reaction and I have the EpiPen. Where exactly do I inject it, how long do I hold it, and can I give another dose if symptoms come back?

Someone got splashed with a possible toxic substance, maybe in the eyes and on clothing. What should we do first to avoid exposing more crew and clean them up?
The patient has a pulse again but looks blue and is breathing badly. How do I decide between just giving oxygen and actively bagging them?
A crewmate says they cannot breathe well, and I need to quickly report useful vitals to the Surgeon. What should I measure first and how?
What equipment is needed for an intraosseous device insertion?
What equipment is needed for treating a choking patient?
What are the main items and tools needed to treat difficulty breathing?
What is the objective of the toxic exposure procedure and what supplies are listed for it?
What tools are needed to perform a mouth exam and send photos to the Surgeon?
